title: Python Execution, Tools, and Evidence · Independent Program Trial
summary: Build and diagnose a small command-line learning log across REPL, script, editor, and terminal workflows. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a reproducible command-line learning log with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain how source, the interpreter, objects, and standard streams participate when a Python program runs.
Choose REPL, script, module, or notebook execution based on the task and reproducibility needs.
Read syntax errors and tracebacks from the final exception back to the relevant source cause.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-basics cumulative stakeholder program."""

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
